steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage says scientists isolated many elements quickly, then introduces a contrasting case: "this wasn't the case with all elements." The blank begins a sentence that acknowledges the general rule but carves out an exception to it. The transition must signal concession — "yes, that's true in general, but not always."

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — For this reason,: signals a result or consequence of the previous sentence. The following sentence is not a result of scientists isolating elements quickly — it contradicts that. ✗
  • Choice B — Granted,: signals concession — you acknowledge the prior claim but introduce an exception. That matches exactly: granted that many elements were isolated quickly, this wasn't true for all of them. ✓
  • Choice C — Likewise,: signals similarity or continuation. The following sentence presents a contrasting case, not a similar one. ✗
  • Choice D — In other words,: signals a restatement or clarification of what was just said. The following sentence does not restate the first sentence; it contradicts it. ✗
That is the correct answer.
